details:
After reconciling the three abandoned stale claims (rows 21/22/25, dead
controllers ctrl-vm-4024 / ctrl-vm-4026) back into the pool, the entire
highest-priority eligible tier (P0, 3 rows) is queue-"CLEAN" (no active
overlaps, no unmet deps) yet none is safely advanceable by an autonomous,
build-less controller:

- r21 #669 "Harden CI validation authority for workflow changes" and
  r25 #673 "Harden Windows CI tool and vcpkg cache validation" are
  control-plane self-modification: their target files are the shared CI
  merge-gate and controller governance themselves -- .github/workflows/build.yml,
  AGENTS.md, scripts/poll_pr_checks.py, scripts/ci_change_classifier.py.
  Every fleet controller and every in-flight worker PR (6 open at time of
  writing) is gated by exactly these files. Rewriting them mid-run risks
  breaking the CI gate for all concurrent PRs, and no single autonomous
  controller can validate that blast radius.

- r22 #670 "Define and enforce the Python resource-plugin trust boundary"
  is native (src/core/CLoader.cpp + res/game.py sandbox). Its own validation
  clause requires "normal Nouraajd/plugin load tests" to pass, but those are
  red main-wide due to the unresolved resource_unit_tests plugin-load break
  (previously recorded). A trust-boundary enforcement change cannot be
  validated on a baseline where normal plugin loading already fails.

Net: the queue is offering controllers their own control plane as P0
implementation work plus a security row gated on a pre-existing baseline
break. The mechanical "CLEAN" shortlist flag does not capture either gate,
so the auto-selection points controllers at structurally unclaimable work.

evidence.issue: #669 Harden CI validation authority for workflow changes

evidence.gate: control-plane / Windows-only CI infra, unvalidatable build-less
evidence.issue: #673 Harden Windows CI tool and vcpkg cache validation

evidence.gate: native CLoader.cpp + game.py; validation requires normal plugin-load tests which are red main-wide (resource_unit_tests break)
evidence.issue: #670 Define and enforce the Python resource-plugin trust boundary

evidence.reconciliation: rows 21/22/25 returned to pool via PRs #1236/#1257/#1258

impact: Auto-selection points build-less controllers at structurally unadvanceable top-tier work; wasted claim/block cycles; P0 tier stalls until the baseline break is fixed or rows go to a privileged/supervised implementer
suggestedImprovement: Mark control-plane self-modification rows (build.yml/AGENTS.md/poller/classifier) and rows whose validation depends on a currently-red baseline as not-auto-selectable for autonomous controllers; route to a privileged/supervised lane; fix resource_unit_tests plugin-load break before #670
summary: Top P0 tier is queue-CLEAN but unclaimable: 2 rows self-modify the shared CI gate/AGENTS.md, 1 is a native plugin trust-boundary gated on the main-wide resource_unit_tests break
